title: Prediction Market Microstructure and Informed Trading
abstract: We study prediction market microstructure with a focus on informed trading patterns. Using a dataset of 10,000 markets, we apply regression analysis to model adverse selection. The DOI for this work is 10.1234/pm.2023.001 and the arXiv identifier is arXiv:2301.12345. Our sample size is 1.2M contracts with p-value < 0.001 for all key results.
body_text:
We study prediction market microstructure with a focus on informed trading patterns.

## Introduction

Prediction markets have been shown to aggregate information effectively. Our methodology applies regression to estimate price impact. The arXiv:2301.12345 paper extends prior work on market microstructure.

## Data

Dataset covers 2021-2023, sample size N=1,200,000. Contains DOI 10.1234/pm.2023.001 for citation. p-value < 0.001 across all hypothesis tests. Confidence interval: [0.23, 0.31]. Experiment design uses control group of random traders.

## Methodology

We use regression with SSRN:3456789 as a companion working paper. Dataset spans multiple prediction market venues including Polymarket.

## Results

Informed traders earn 3.2% above market baseline. Methodology validated on holdout dataset.
